name: Cosmic-string GW interpretation of NANOGrav signal
summary: Cosmic strings formed in early-universe symmetry breaking radiate gravitational waves through cusps and kinks on cosmic-string loops; the resulting stochastic background can extend from nanohertz to LIGO-band. A cosmic-string tension G mu ~ 10^-11 - 10^-10 produces a PTA-detectable signal that competes with the SMBHB interpretation of NANOGrav 15-yr. Distinguished from SMBHB by spectral shape (cosmic strings give nearly scale-free spectrum) and anisotropy (cosmic-string signal is highly isotropic).

addresses_puzzles.note: Cosmic-string tension G mu ~ 10^-10 produces stochastic GW background consistent with NANOGrav amplitude. Spectral index for cusp+kink radiation is closer to f^0 than SMBHB f^-2/3; current NANOGrav posterior accommodates both. Metastable cosmic strings (those that decay via monopole pair-production) give modified spectrum that fits NANOGrav better.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicted amplitude in NANOGrav-favored range for G mu ~ 10^-11 - 10^-10. CMB-S4 constraints (G mu < 10^-7) easily satisfied.
CMB anisotropy from cosmic strings at G mu < 10^-7 is subdominant to inflationary perturbations.

new_fields: U(1) gauge field producing cosmic strings
introduces.note: Single new free parameter: string tension G mu, set by the U(1)-breaking scale.

history: Cosmic-string GW from cusps/kinks: Vilenkin (1981); modern numerical work Blanco-Pillado-Olum-Shlaer (2014). NANOGrav interpretation: Ellis, Lewicki (2021, arXiv:2009.06555), 'Cosmic string interpretation of NANOGrav pulsar timing data'. Metastable-string variant: Buchmuller, Domcke, Murayama, Schmitz (2020), arXiv:1908.03227. Competing with SMBHB interpretation for the 2023 signal.

references.citation: Ellis and Lewicki, 'Cosmic string interpretation of NANOGrav pulsar timing data', Phys. Rev. Lett. 126, 041304 (2021)
